articles.title: Rain relief likely in Karnataka amid heatwave; Thunderstorms forecast across regions - newsfirstprime.com
articles.full_text:
IMD issues yellow alert for 15 districts and orange alert for 5; gusty winds, lightning and scattered rain expected across interior and coastal Karnataka.
After days of intense summer heat across Karnataka, there is some relief in sight for residents. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted rainfall in several parts of the state today, bringing hope to people struggling with rising temperatures.
According to the latest forecast, a total of 15 districts have been placed under a yellow alert, while 5 districts are under an orange alert due to the possibility of more intense weather activity. The development comes as a welcome update for citizens affected by the scorching summer conditions.
Weather officials have indicated that thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and strong winds are likely in multiple regions. Wind speeds are expected to reach between 30 and 40 kmph in some areas, especially during the afternoon and evening hours.

The sudden change in weather is attributed to a drop in air pressure at around 1.5 km above sea level over the interior parts of Karnataka. This shift in atmospheric conditions is expected to trigger unseasonal rainfall across north interior, south interior and coastal regions of the state.
Districts including Chikkamagaluru, Hassan, Kodagu and Shivamogga are likely to receive moderate rain along with thunder and lightning later in the day. These areas may also experience gusty winds.
Meanwhile, lighter rainfall is expected in districts such as Belagavi, Dharwad, Gadag, Haveri, Vijayapura and Bagalkot. Coastal regions including Dakshina Kannada, Udupi and Uttara Kannada are also expected to witness light showers.
The IMD has advised people to stay cautious, especially during thunderstorms and strong winds. With the possibility of rain across many regions, residents can expect a slight drop in temperatures and some relief from the ongoing heat.
